title.rendered: Why Switching from Your Bank Could Save You Hundreds on International Transfers

content.rendered:
If you’re still using your bank or even one of the new online “neo-banks” to send money abroad, you could be paying more than you think.

It’s easy to assume your regular or app-based bank offers fair exchange rates and low fees. But the truth is, most financial institutions, traditional or modern, still build hidden costs into their rates. Those small margins can quickly add up.

At Travel FX, we believe sending money abroad should be simple, transparent, and great value without losing pounds in the process.

The hidden cost of convenience

Banks and neo-banks make sending money overseas convenient, but convenience often comes at a price.

When you transfer money through them, you usually pay in two ways:

  1. Uncompetitive exchange rates – Many banks and even app-based providers apply a hidden markup of 2–5% on top of the true market rate.
  2. Transfer or service fees – High-street banks often charge £20–£40 per transaction, and some neo-banks add fees for instant or weekend transfers.

For example: Sending £5,000 abroad with a 3% margin means you lose around £150 immediately, before any transfer charges are added.

Repeat that a few times a year for property payments, family support, or travel expenses, and you could be out hundreds of pounds in unnecessary costs.

Even online banks aren’t always transparent

Neo-banks and online platforms have changed the way we move money, but not always how much it costs.

Many promote “low fees” and “real rates”, yet:

  • They may still use their own internal pricing rather than the pure market rate.
  • They often apply mark-ups that aren’t obvious until you check the total received.
  • Fees can vary by currency, amount, or speed of delivery.

So while they’re quicker and more user-friendly than old-school banks, they don’t always offer the best overall value.

Real savings in action

Let’s look at a real-world example.

You’re sending £5,000 to Spain to pay for accommodation, a holiday home, or to help family.

Here’s how it breaks down:

  • Market (interbank) rate: ~€1.1450 per £1*
  • Travel FX rate: €1.1340 per £1
  • Typical bank rate (3% margin): ~€1.1116 per £1
  • Typical neo-bank or app rate (1.5% margin): ~€1.1273 per £1
Provider Approx. rate € received for £5,000 Difference vs Travel FX
Market rate (~€1.1450) €1.1450 €5,725 N/A (not available to consumers)
Travel FX €1.1340 €5,670 —
Neo-bank/app €1.1273 €5,637 –€33
High-street bank €1.1116 €5,558 –€112

With Travel FX, you’d receive around €5,670, compared to roughly €5,558 from a high-street bank, meaning you could save €112 (about £98) on just one transaction. Even against a popular neo-bank, you’d still come out ahead by about €33 (£29).

What is the “market rate”?

The market rate, also known as the interbank rate, is the real exchange rate used by banks and major financial institutions when trading currencies between themselves. It’s the purest, most accurate reflection of a currency’s value, sitting exactly between the buy and sell prices.

However, this rate isn’t available to the general public. Every retail bank, money app, or transfer company adds a small margin to make a profit and cover operational costs. That’s why you’ll never get the full interbank rate on a personal transfer, but you can get closer to it with the right provider.

At Travel FX, we keep that margin tight and display our live rates transparently. You’ll always see exactly what you’re getting before confirming your transfer, with no hidden fees, no vague estimates, and no nasty surprises.

How Travel FX helps you save

We’ve been helping UK customers make smarter currency decisions for over a decade. Here’s how we make it easy to send money abroad without overpaying:

  • 💸 Better exchange rates: Our rates often beat banks and even the leading money apps.
  • 🚫 No hidden fees: What you send is what arrives, with full transparency.
  • ⚡ Fast, reliable transfers: Quick and secure payments, every time.
  • 🇬🇧 UK-based and trusted: Decades of experience in foreign exchange and a long-standing reputation for reliability.

Whether you’re transferring money to pay for a holiday home, support family overseas, or settle bills abroad, Travel FX ensures your money goes further.

Smarter, simpler, fairer

Switching from your bank or app doesn’t just save you money, it gives you control and clarity. With Travel FX, you always know:

  • The exact rate you’re getting
  • How much your recipient will receive
  • That your transfer is handled safely and efficiently

No hidden costs, no inflated margins, just a smarter way to send money abroad.

Ready to make the switch?

Stop letting banks and money apps take a bigger slice of your hard-earned cash.
 Join thousands of customers who trust Travel FX for fast, secure, and great-value international transfers.

 Start your transfer today

Note:

Market rate sourced from PoundSterlingLive.com on 27 October 2025. Exchange rates fluctuate and may vary at the time of transfer.  Travel FX rate sourced on on 27 October 2025.

itemListElement.name: Why Switching from Your Bank Could Save You Hundreds on International Transfers

@graph.name: Travel FX Blog
@graph.description: Travel money news and tips from Travel FX

@graph.name: Lucy Reed
@graph.description: Hi! I’m Lucy, as well as writing blog posts I lead Customer Experience and UX at Travel FX. With over 15 years’ experience in the foreign exchange industry, I specialise in helping UK travellers understand exchange rates, avoid unnecessary fees, and feel confident about their travel money before they fly. Having lived both in the UK and overseas, and travelled extensively over the years, I understand first-hand how important it is to get the details right when you’re planning a trip. From managing your travel budget to choosing the right foreign currency or travel money card, small decisions can make a big difference.
